title: Pachyloma marginata
intro: Pachyloma marginata, commonly known as Pachyloma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pachyloma marginata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pachyloma is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Pachyloma marginata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant is native to South Africa and is also known as a shrub. The classification of Pachyloma marginata is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Oxalidales. Within the family Cunoniaceae, Pachyloma marginata is part of the genus Pachyloma. The plant's classification is an important part of its identity and is used in botanical research and identification.
